title: Pipeline Quality Gates — 5 Surgical Prompt Edits
description: Make 5 targeted edits to 4 pipeline docs to prevent silent requirement drops, enforce user-centric DOD for UI work, and reweight reviews toward acceptance testing.

scope.in: Edit planner-prompt.md (COO clarification), 07b-project-brainstorm.md (user-centric DOD), 09-execute-projects.md (acceptance review + default-state verification), scope-and-dod.md (DOD rules)
scope.out: Context trim (separate directive), builder self-verification (dropped), new schemas/steps/scripts

dod.criterion: COO planning prompt includes clarification/gap-surfacing behavior for ambiguous requirements
Project brainstorm prompt includes user-centric DOD guidance for UI tasks with good/bad examples
Review prompt reweighted to prioritize acceptance testing over code review
Review instructions include default-state verification requirement for UI tasks
scope-and-dod.md includes UI/Visual DOD rules section

tasks.title: Add clarification behavior to COO planning prompt
tasks.user_scenario: When the CEO issues a directive with ambiguous requirements, the COO asks clarifying questions instead of silently compressing scope.
tasks.scope: Edit planner-prompt.md to add a clarification/gap-surfacing section after 'CHALLENGE FIRST' and before 'CRITICAL OUTPUT FORMAT'.

dod.criterion: planner-prompt.md contains a new section between CHALLENGE FIRST and CRITICAL OUTPUT FORMAT that instructs the COO to identify ambiguous or under-specified requirements
The new section gives 2-3 concrete examples of ambiguity patterns
The instruction tells the COO to surface questions in the challenges.risks array rather than introducing new schema fields
The existing JSON output schema in planner-prompt.md is unchanged — no new fields added

tasks.title: Add user-centric DOD guidance for UI tasks to project brainstorm
tasks.user_scenario: When the CTO decomposes a UI project into tasks, DOD criteria describe what the user sees at default state.
tasks.scope: Edit 07b-project-brainstorm.md to add UI-specific DOD guidance inside TASK DECOMPOSITION RULES.

dod.criterion: 07b-project-brainstorm.md brainstorm prompt contains new guidance within TASK DECOMPOSITION RULES requiring UI task DOD to describe what user sees at default state
The guidance includes at least 2 contrasting examples: bad UI DOD vs good UI DOD
The guidance is scoped to UI/visual tasks only
No changes to the JSON output schema or process steps outside the brainstorm prompt

tasks.title: Reweight review prompt toward acceptance testing over code review
tasks.user_scenario: Reviewers spend proportionally more effort verifying user experience than checking code quality.
tasks.scope: Edit 09-execute-projects.md User-Perspective Review section. Reweight so acceptance testing is PRIMARY.

dod.criterion: The reviewer prompt leads with user_scenario verification and DOD checking before code quality
The prompt explicitly states that failing user_scenario verification = review_outcome 'fail'
workflow_improvement 'no' is sufficient for 'fail' even if code quality is clean
The code-review phase prompt is NOT modified

tasks.title: Add default-state verification requirement to review instructions
tasks.user_scenario: Reviewers explicitly verify UI changes at default zoom, default view, default data.
tasks.scope: Edit 09-execute-projects.md Review completeness section. Add default-state verification for UI tasks.

dod.criterion: Review completeness section includes a default_state_check field in review JSON output
Prompt instructs reviewers to verify at default zoom (100%), default view, representative data
default_state_check is required only for UI file tasks — backend-only tasks skip it
Prompt gives a concrete example of what default-state verification catches

tasks.title: Add user-centric DOD rules for UI/visual work to scope-and-dod.md
tasks.user_scenario: Pipeline participants referencing DOD rules see explicit guidance for UI task DOD.
tasks.scope: Edit scope-and-dod.md to add new section after 'Definition of Done Rules'.

dod.criterion: scope-and-dod.md contains a new section for UI/Visual DOD rules
Section includes at least 2 contrasting examples: bad vs good UI DOD
Rules specify default-state conditions: default zoom, default view, representative data
New section extends general DOD rules without contradiction
